text:
Market today: Tech is getting a lift from Meta's AI monetization pop - that's the real signal today. When a mega-cap proves AI infrastructure has a revenue model, it drags sentiment across cloud, chips, and software. Your names benefit from that tailwind. Nasdaq concentration in 10 stocks (headline 9) reminds us the rally is narrow - that's fine while you're in the right names, but it's fragile.

Your holdings: SOXL (-6.79%) is the one to watch - it's a 3x leveraged chip ETF that decays daily, and drawdowns hit harder on margin. Stop is $200, price is $237, not in danger yet but it bleeds quietly. SPAL (-8.65%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO - already down and stops are tight at $21.38; margin amplifies every move here, be honest about size. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec down hard - know your exit.

Watch: Whether the AI-monetization theme from Meta spreads into chip names today. Also watch Fed language - Warsh's warning means no rate-cut hints, which can cap multiple expansion.

Bottom line: Stay long your quality names, but keep a close eye on your two leveraged ETFs - one bad day can accelerate fast.
plays: SPAL: Stop is $21.38 with price at $22.98 - if it breaks $21.38, exit without hesitation; leveraged and on margin means losses compound quickly.
PTNM: Down 15.25% as a tiny spec - decide today what your real exit level is; no scanner setup supports adding here.
No new buys today - Meta's AI news is bullish mood music but not a specific entry signal for any scanner setup that's also actionable right now; hold and watch.
